messages.content:
Which statement is TRUE about Shays' Rebellion? A. The state government could not intervene to stop it (not allowed).
B. The militia from a different nation had to end the rebellion under the rules of the Articles.
C. The state could not create a militia to help under the rules of the Articles.
D. The federal government could not intervene in the rebellion in order to stop it (not allowed).
A. The state government could not intervene to stop it (not allowed).

Which of the following favored the smaller states over the larger ones?
A. the Virginia Plan
B. the Connecticut Compromise
C. the New Jersey Plan
C. the New Jersey Plan

During his farewell address in 1796, who warned against creating American political A. John Adams
parties?
B. George Washington
C. Thomas Jefferson
B. George Washington

What were the "Federalist Papers" supposed to do? A. Announce the ratification of the Constitution to the public.
B. Inform the public of the need for a stronger central government.
C. Warn the public of the corruption of the central government.
B. Inform the public of the need for a stronger central government.

Which of the following is a power NOT granted to Congress according to Article 1 of the Constitution?
A. The power to veto laws
B. The power to declare war
C. The power to collect taxes
A. The power to veto laws (This power is granted to the president, not Congress)

All of the following are roles of the president EXCEPT A. Chief Executive - enforcing the law
B. Chief Diplomat - representing the US abroad
C. Commander in Chief - controlling the military
D. Chief Justice - judging whether laws are constitutional
D. Chief Justice - judging whether laws are constitutional (This role is the power of the Supreme Court, not the president)

According to Article 3 of the Constitution, what is the duty of the Supreme Court? A. To bring about the goals of the justices' political parties
B. To interpret the Constitution
C. To create and enforce laws
B. To interpret the Constitution

What is required of all states in Article 4 in the Constitution? A. States must give up rule over state law to the Supreme Court.
B. States must agree to one set of laws for all states.
C. States must back the President of the United States at all times.
D. States must honor the rules and laws passed by other states.
D. States must honor the rules and laws passed by other states.

An Amendment cannot be ratified by the state legislatures until it is proposed by which of the following bodies? A. Supreme Court
B. Congress
C. Voters
D. the President
B. Congress

Where must a bill be introduced in order for it to be considered at the federal level? A. Congress
C. Citizens
B. Supreme Court
D. President
A. Congress

A 21-year-old citizen could be elected to which of the following? A. neither House nor Senate
B. either House or Senate
C. House of
Representatives
D. Senate
C. House of Representatives

Which statement is NOT TRUE? A. Federal judges are NOT required to be natural born citizens.
B. Federal judges can only serve a maximum of two terms.
C. Federal judges have NO term limit.
B. Federal judges can only serve a maximum of two terms. (There are no term limits for federal judges)

What does it mean to formally "impeach" a president? A. The president is praised for their actions.
B. Criminal charges are brought against him/her.
C. The president is removed from office.
B. Criminal charges are brought against him/her.

Which state is NOT a winner- take-all state for electoral votes, but instead assigns proportional votes to both candidates? A. Missouri
B. Alaska
C. Maine
C. Maine

The party that holds the
most seats in the House of Representatives has a leader who is known as what?
A. executive official
B. majority leader
C. minority leader
D. legislative leader
B. Majority leader

Which of the following in the presidential line of succession is from
the Senate?
UNITED
A. Attorney General
C. Secretary of State
B. President Pro Tempore
D. Secretary of Treasury
B. President Pro Tempore.
